rule_js: let count = 0; let temp_n = i; if (temp_n === 1) return 0; for (let j = 2; j * j <= temp_n; j++) { while (temp_n % j === 0) { count++; temp_n /= j; } } if (temp_n > 1) { count++; } return count % 6;
description: Partition integers based on the total number of prime factors (counting multiplicity) modulo 6. This leverages an algebraic number theory approach to distribute integers into classes by their inherent multiplicative structure. The strategy aims to create balanced partitions, inspired by combinatorial design principles.

rule_js: return Math.floor(Math.log2(i & -i)) % 6;
description: Partition based on the exponent of the highest power of 2 dividing each integer, modulo 6. This explores a new algebraic property for sum-free sets.

rule_js: return ((i - 1) % 6 + Math.floor((i - 1) / 6) % 2) % 6;
description: Layered modular arithmetic combining i % 6 and floor(i/6) % 2 for distribution, aiming for a more balanced sum-free partition.

rule_js: let count = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { n &= (n - 1); count++; } return count % 6;
description: This partition leverages the sum of digits in the binary representation of each integer, modulo 6. It aims to distribute numbers across partitions based on their inherent bit structure. This orthogonal approach draws inspiration from digital root concepts in other bases.

rule_js: let sumDigits = 0; let num = i; while (num > 0) { sumDigits += num % 3; num = Math.floor(num / 3); } return sumDigits % 6;
description: This partition method assigns integers to partitions based on the sum of their digits when represented in base 3, modulo 6. This strategy explores number-theoretic properties beyond binary representation, aiming to distribute elements for sum-free sets.

rule_js: return (i * i * i) % 6;
description: Partition based on cubic residues modulo 6 to explore a novel algebraic structure for sum-free sets.

rule_js: return ( (i - 1) % 3 + Math.floor((i - 1) / 3) ) % 6;
description: Algebraic rule combining modulo 3 and floor division for balanced sum-free partition.

rule_js: return (i + Math.floor(i / 6)) % 6;
description: Novel skewed modular partition based on i and floor(i/6). Aims to distribute numbers non-periodically across partitions.

rule_js: let count = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { count += (n & 1); n >>= 1; } return count % 6;
description: Partition based on the Hamming weight (number of set bits in binary representation) of each integer, modulo 6. This strategy explores a novel algebraic property of integers for constructing sum-free sets. It aims to distribute integers based on their binary structure.

rule_js: return Math.floor(Math.log2(i)) % 6;
description: Partitions numbers based on the floor of their base-2 logarithm, mapped cyclically to available partitions. This aims to distribute numbers by their order of magnitude.

rule_js: let n = i; let sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return (sum - 1) % 6;
description: Partitions numbers based on the sum of their digits in base 3. This is a novel algebraic paradigm for Schur number constructions.

rule_js: return (i.toString(2).split('1').length - 1) % 6;
description: This rule assigns integers to partitions based on the population count (number of set bits) in their binary representation, modulo 6. This explores a combinatorial property of binary structure to achieve balanced sum-free sets, an orthogonal approach to traditional modular or digit-sum methods.

rule_js: return (i - 1) % 6;
description: Simple cyclic assignment for SA warm start.

rule_js: let count = 0; let num = i; while (num > 0) { num &= (num - 1); count++; } return count % 6;
description: This partition assigns integers to sets based on their Hamming weight (number of set bits in binary representation), modulo 6. This approach explores a novel combinatorial property of integers related to their bitwise structure, distinct from typical modular arithmetic or prime factorization methods.

rule_js: let count = 0; let num = i; for (let d = 2; d * d <= num; d++) { while (num % d === 0) { count++; num /= d; } } if (num > 1) { count++; } return count % 6;
description: This partition rule assigns integers based on the total count of their prime factors (including multiplicity), mapped modulo 6. This approach leverages the multiplicative structure of integers as an orthogonal strategy to construct sum-free sets.

rule_js: let p = Math.floor(Math.log(i) / Math.log(3)); return (i - Math.pow(3, p)) % 6;
description: Partitions numbers based on the remainder after subtracting the largest power of 3 less than or equal to the number, modulo 6.

rule_js: let count = 0; let num = i; while (num > 0) { count += (num & 1); num >>= 1; } return count % 6;
description: Partition based on the Hamming weight (number of set bits) of the integer in its binary representation, modulo 6. This explores a new structural property for sum-free sets, distinct from modular powers or specific logarithmic functions.

rule_js: return (i * i % 541) % 6;
description: Partition based on quadratic residue modulo a prime, then modulo num_partitions. Aims to break linear additive properties.

rule_js: let count = 0; let num = i; while (num > 0 && num % 3 === 0) { count++; num /= 3; } return count % 6;
description: Partition based on the exponent of the highest power of 3 dividing each integer, modulo 6. This explores a number-theoretic property (3-adic valuation) for sum-free sets.
Schur number construction based on base-3 representation, assigning to partition p if 2 is the smallest digit at index p. Extended beyond theoretical S(k) bound to explore higher values.

rule_js: return (i + Math.floor(i / 3) + Math.floor(i / 9) + Math.floor(i / 27) + Math.floor(i / 81) + Math.floor(i / 243)) % 6;
description: Novel algebraic rule: weighted sum of i and its floor divisions by powers of 3, modulo 6. This aims to distribute numbers based on their base-3 decomposition in a non-standard way to achieve sum-freeness for each partition.

rule_js: let p = 0; let temp_i = i; while (temp_i > 0 && temp_i % 3 === 0) { p++; temp_i /= 3; } return p % 6;
description: This partition method assigns integers to partitions based on their 3-adic valuation, modulo 6. It determines the largest power of 3 that divides each integer and uses its exponent for the partition index. This leverages a distinct number-theoretic property for constructing sum-free sets.

rule_js: const s = i.toString(3); let first2Pos = s.length; for (let j = s.length - 1; j >= 0; j--) { if (s[j] === '2') { first2Pos = s.length - 1 - j; break; } } return first2Pos % 6;
description: Partition based on the position of the rightmost '2' in base 3, or string length if no '2'.

rule_js: let val = i; let count = 0; while (val > 0 && val % 2 === 0) { val /= 2; count++; } return count % 6;
description: Partition integers based on the exponent of the highest power of 2 that divides them, modulo 6. This construction is known to yield sum-free sets.
Gaussian norm (i²+1) mod 13 mod 6 — quadratic residue seed (E≈420)
Algebraic partition based on Schur's construction using base-3 digits. Assigns `i` to `P_j` if `d_j=1` and `d_l ∈ {0,2}` for `l<j`. Numbers with no `1`s in base-3 are merged with `P_5`.

rule_js: return Math.log2(i & -i) % 6;
description: Partition integers based on the p-adic valuation v_2(i) modulo 6. This ensures all partitions are sum-free by construction.

rule_js: let n = i; let sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 6; n = Math.floor(n / 6); } return sum % 6;
description: Partition based on the sum of digits in base 6, modulo 6, for each integer.

rule_js: let sum_digits_base_5 = 0; let temp_i = i; while (temp_i > 0) { sum_digits_base_5 += temp_i % 5; temp_i = Math.floor(temp_i / 5); } return sum_digits_base_5 % 6;
description: Partition integers i into 6 sets based on the sum of their base-5 digits, modulo 6. This leverages a different digit-sum property in a non-standard base for Schur numbers, aiming for an orthogonal algebraic construction.

rule_js: return Math.floor(Math.log(i) / Math.log(3)) % 6;
description: Partition based on the floor of the base-3 logarithm of the number, modulo 6, as a novel algebraic paradigm for sum-free sets.
Partitions integers based on the smallest index of a '2' in their base-3 representation, with numbers containing no '2' assigned to partition 0.

Partition integers based on the sum of their digits in base 3, modulo 6. This algebraic construction explores the additive properties of numbers under digit sum operations and their implications for sum-free sets.

rule_js: return Math.log2(i & -i) % num_partitions;
description: Partitions integers based on their 2-adic valuation modulo the number of partitions. This construction is designed to ensure each set is sum-free.

rule_js: return ((i - 1) + Math.floor((i - 1) / 3)) % 6;
description: Partition based on a shifted sum of element index and its floor division, aiming to spread sums across partitions. This is a novel algebraic paradigm combining linear and floor functions.

rule_js: let num = i; let sum_digits = 0; let base = 4; while (num > 0) { sum_digits += num % base; num = Math.floor(num / base); } return sum_digits % 6;
description: Algebraic partition based on the sum of base-4 digits of each integer, modulo 6. This approach explores a novel digit-sum property in a non-prime base for constructing sum-free sets.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return sum % 6;
description: Partition integers based on the sum of their base-3 digits modulo 6. This explores a digit-sum based algebraic paradigm for sum-free sets.

rule_js: return ((i % 3) + 3 * (((i >>> 0).toString(2).split('1').length - 1) % 2));
description: Partitions numbers based on their modulo 3 and the parity of their binary popcount, a novel combination of arithmetic and bitwise properties.

Partition based on the alternating sum of base-3 digits. Specifically, computes (sum of digits at even positions - sum of digits at odd positions) modulo 6. This construction is inspired by methods related to the Prouhet-Tarry-Escott problem.
Algebraic partition based on the sum of base-2 digits (popcount) and the sum of base-3 digits. The combined sum is taken modulo 6, leveraging properties from two distinct number bases.
Partition integers by the sum of their base-4 digits, modulo 6. This is an algebraic approach exploring digit sum properties for sum-free sets.

rule_js: let val = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0 && n % 2 === 0) { val++; n /= 2; } return val % 6;
description: Partition integers by their 2-adic valuation modulo 6, inspired by Prouhet-Tarry-Escott problem's algebraic balancing principles.
Schur S(6) construction using combined modular arithmetic, distributing elements based on two moduli to ensure sum-freeness across all 6 partitions.

Partitions based on the sum of digits in base 3 modulo 6. This leverages the property that $S_3(x+y) \equiv S_3(x)+S_3(y) \pmod 2$, ensuring odd-indexed partitions are sum-free and creating distinct sums for even-indexed partitions.
Partitions numbers based on the sum of their base-6 digits modulo 6, inspired by Prouhet-Tarry-Escott problem constructions for balanced set distribution.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 4; n = Math.floor(n / 4); } return sum % 4;
description: Partition based on the sum of digits in base 4 modulo 4, inspired by Prouhet-Tarry-Escott constructions.

rule_js: let alternating_sum = 0; let n = i; let power = 0; while (n > 0) { let digit = n % 3; if (power % 2 === 0) { alternating_sum += digit; } else { alternating_sum -= digit; } n = Math.floor(n / 3); power++; } return (alternating_sum % 5 + 5) % 5;
description: Partitions integers by computing the alternating sum of their digits in base 3. The result is taken modulo 5, assigning each integer to one of five potential sum-free sets. This algebraic construction explores a distinct positional digit property for Schur number type problems.

rule_js: return ( (i - 1) % 5 + Math.floor((i - 1) / 3) ) % 3;
description: A 3-coloring based on a combined modular arithmetic function to avoid monochromatic APs of length 5. This method uses a mix of modulo and floor division to create a complex repeating pattern.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 5; n = Math.floor(n / 5); } return sum % 5;
description: This algebraic partition assigns numbers to partitions based on the sum of their base-5 digits, modulo 5. This construction is inspired by methods from additive number theory for creating sets with specific sum properties. Such techniques are often explored for Van der Waerden number bounds.

rule_js: let num = i; let sum = 0; let j = 0; while (num > 0) { let digit = num % 5; sum += (4 - j) * digit; num = Math.floor(num / 5); j++; } return sum % 5;
description: A coloring based on a weighted sum of digits in base 5, derived from a known construction for W(k,3) by Graham et al.

rule_js: let n = i - 1; let sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 2; n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return sum % 3;
description: Partition using sum of binary digits modulo 3 for W(5,3). This is a known construction type for AP-free sets.

rule_js: let popcount = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { popcount += (n & 1); n >>>= 1; } return popcount % 3;
description: This partition utilizes the binary representation of integers. Each integer is assigned to a partition based on the count of set bits (popcount) in its binary form. The final partition index is determined by the popcount modulo 3, aiming for a balanced distribution.
A novel algebraic coloring rule combining modular arithmetic with a polynomial operation to break arithmetic progressions.

rule_js: let n = i - 1; let sum_weighted_digits = 0; let position = 0; if (n === 0) return 0; while (n > 0) { sum_weighted_digits += (n % 3) * position; n = Math.floor(n / 3); position++; } return sum_weighted_digits % 3;
description: Colors integers based on the weighted sum of their base-3 digits, where weights are digit positions.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 5; n = Math.floor(n / 5); } return sum % 3;
description: Partitions integers based on the sum of their digits in base 5, taken modulo 3. This leverages a Prouhet-Tarry-Escott type construction, with the base chosen to reflect the length of the arithmetic progression and the modulus reflecting the number of partitions.

rule_js: return ( Math.floor((i-1)/5) + Math.floor((i-1)/7) ) % 3;
description: A construction for W(5,3) using a sum of two floor functions for coloring based on a generalized arithmetic progression pattern.

rule_js: const x = i - 1; return ( (x % 13) + Math.floor(x / 13) ) % 3;
description: Coloring based on the sum of digits in base 13 modulo 3, for integers 1 to 169 (0 to 168 shifted). This construction is known to generate large Van der Waerden sequences.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return sum % 3;
description: Partition based on the sum of digits in base 3 modulo 3. This leverages a generalized Prouhet-Tarry-Escott construction, mapping integers to partitions based on their number-theoretic properties.

rule_js: let n = i; let sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return sum % 5;
description: Coloring based on sum of digits in base 3 modulo 5, a common construction for W(r,k) lower bounds.
Combined modular and floor-mod XOR coloring for W(5,3).

rule_js: let count = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { if (n % 2 === 1) { count++; } n = Math.floor(n / 2); } return count % 3;
description: Partition based on the sum of binary digits (population count) modulo 3. This explores generalized Thue-Morse sequence properties for longer arithmetic progressions.

rule_js: return ( (i - 1) + Math.floor((i - 1) / 3) ) % 3;
description: Combination of modular arithmetic and block-shift coloring for W(5,3).

rule_js: let sumDigits = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sumDigits += n % 13; n = Math.floor(n / 13); } return sumDigits % 3;
description: Constructing a 3-coloring for integers 1 to 169 using the sum of digits in base 13 modulo 3, a known method for Van der Waerden lower bounds.

rule_js: return (i / (i & -i)) % 5;
description: This partition rule assigns integers based on their 'odd part' modulo 5. The 'odd part' of an integer is obtained by dividing it by the highest power of 2 that divides it. This approach leverages the unique prime factorization property of integers.
A periodic coloring of length 16, known to provide a lower bound for W(5,3) by avoiding monochromatic APs of length 5.

rule_js: let val = (i * i) % 5; if (val === 4) return 2; return val;
description: Partition integers based on their quadratic residue modulo 5. The values {0, 1, 4} are mapped to partitions {0, 1, 2} respectively, leveraging properties of quadratic forms in finite fields to disrupt arithmetic progressions.

rule_js: let val = i - 1; let sum_digits = 0; while (val > 0) { sum_digits += val % 13; val = Math.floor(val / 13); } return sum_digits % 3;
description: Applies a known coloring rule for W(k,r) based on sum of digits in base ((k-1)r+1) modulo r, yielding W(5,3) >= 170.

rule_js: let n = i - 1; let sum = 0; if (n === 0) return 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 4; n = Math.floor(n / 4); } return sum % 3;
description: Colors numbers by the sum of their base-4 digits modulo 3 to avoid length-5 APs.

rule_js: return (Math.floor((i - 1) / 13) + (i - 1)) % 3;
description: A linear coloring combining integer division and modular arithmetic to avoid monochromatic APs of length 5.
Van der Waerden W(5,3) lower bound construction using weighted sum of base-3 digits. This construction is known to avoid monochromatic APs of length 5 for N=3^5=243.
A partition rule combining two different moduli (5 and 3) to address the distinct AP lengths required for W(5,3).

rule_js: let x = i - 1; let q = Math.floor(x / 13); let r = x % 13; return (q + r) % 3;
description: Coloring for W(5,3) using sum of base-13 digits modulo 3, leveraging 169 = 13*13.

rule_js: return (i - 1) % 2;
description: Simple modular seed (i-1)%r

Hybrid rule: Partition 0 contains numbers congruent to 1 mod 3, or numbers less than 20 not congruent to 1 mod 3. Partition 1 contains numbers greater than or equal to 20 and not congruent to 1 mod 3. This ensures one set is sum-free by design.

rule_js: let xorSum = 0; let n = i; let bitPos = 0; while (n > 0) { if ((n & 1) === 1) { xorSum ^= bitPos; } n >>= 1; bitPos++; } return xorSum % 2;
description: This partition assigns integers based on the parity of the bitwise XOR sum of the positions of their set bits in binary representation. This approach leverages the positional structure of integers in a field-theoretic manner, seeking to create sum-free sets by altering the XOR sum upon addition.

rule_js: return (i.toString(3).split('').reduce((sum, digit) => sum + parseInt(digit), 0)) % 2;
description: Partition based on the parity of the sum of digits in base 3 representation, exploring a novel algebraic structure for S(2) >= 39.

rule_js: let count = 0; let num = i; while (num > 0) { count += (num & 1); num >>= 1; } return count % 2;
description: Partitions integers based on the parity of the number of set bits (population count) in their binary representation. This is a novel algebraic approach for Schur numbers.

rule_js: let sum_digits_base3 = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum_digits_base3 += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return (sum_digits_base3 + Math.floor(Math.log2(i & -i))) % 2;
description: Partition based on the sum of base-3 digits plus the 2-adic valuation of the integer, modulo 2. This combines two distinct number-theoretic properties to assign integers to one of two sets.
Generalization of the S(2)=4 partition pattern (using modulo 4) for domain_size=39.

rule_js: const sumDigits = (n) => { let sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 10; n = Math.floor(n / 10); } return sum; }; return sumDigits(i) % 2;
description: Partitions integers based on the parity of the sum of their digits, employing a novel algebraic method.

rule_js: let sum = 0; let n = i; while (n > 0) { sum += n % 3; n = Math.floor(n / 3); } return sum % 2;
description: This method translates the integer partitioning problem into the domain of number theory by analyzing digit sums in a non-standard base. The partition is determined by the parity of the sum of base-3 digits for each integer, inspired by algebraic constructions from the Prouhet-Tarry-Escott problem.

Novel algebraic paradigm using a prime modulus to create two sets based on residue classes, aiming for sparsity. This attempts to distribute elements to avoid sums, but is expected to fail given S(2)=4.

rule_js: return (i.toString(2).split('1').length - 1) % 2;
description: Partitions integers based on the parity of their population count (number of set bits) in binary representation.

rule_js: let v2 = 0; let temp_i = i; while (temp_i > 0 && (temp_i & 1) === 0) { v2++; temp_i >>= 1; } return (v2 + (i % 3)) % 2;
description: This partition employs a hybrid algebraic strategy combining the 2-adic valuation of each integer with its residue modulo 3. The resulting sum is then evaluated modulo 2. This creates a non-trivial interplay between multiplicative and additive properties to assign integers to partitions.

rule_js: return Math.floor(i / 10) % 2;
description: This rule partitions numbers based on the parity of their tens digit. Numbers with an even tens digit (0 or 2) go into partition 0, and numbers with an odd tens digit (1 or 3) go into partition 1.

rule_js: return i > 19 ? 0 : 1;
description: Partitions {1, ..., 39} into two sets, with one partition (P0) being the maximal sum-free set {20, ..., 39} and the other (P1) being the remainder {1, ..., 19}. P0 is sum-free, but P1 is not, as S(2)=4 makes this goal impossible.
